steps.title: Identify the drawback's root cause
steps.content:
The passage establishes that the autobiographical label creates “drawbacks” specifically because “high value” is placed on “creativity and imagination” in art.

When readers interpret the novel as “merely fictionalized” events—constrained by fact rather than imagination—they face this stated drawback.

steps.title: Match the inference to the choices
steps.content:
If readers see the work as just reworking real events instead of creating imaginatively, they would logically conclude it deserves less praise than a fully imaginative work.

The other choices address critic disagreement, identifying characters' real-world sources, or the author's responsibility—none follow from the core concern about perceived creativity.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
